labels.value: No fee due date is configured for the associated {0} of {1}. Please ensure that a valid due date is set to proceed.

labels.shortDescription: Error Generate New Transaction Order Missing Due Date
labels.value: No fee due date is configured for the {0} associated to {1}. Please ensure that a valid due date is set to proceed.

labels.shortDescription: Error Generate New Transaction Order Missing Student Fee Type
labels.value: No student fee type is configured for the {0}. Please ensure that a valid student fee type is set to proceed.

labels.value: Unable to initialize the job generator with class name {0}. Please ensure the class exists and declared with global modifier.

You are not allowed to change the status back to a non-Create {0} status when there are existing {0} linked to the {1}. Please fix or remove the related {0} if you need to re-perform the approval process.
No master {0} is set in the {1}, and no default {0} is found for the {2} or {3}. A {0} is required for an {4} with an 'In Progress' status.
You are not allowed to change the status back to 'Draft' or 'Preview', nor can you change the status from 'Preview' to another status.

labels.shortDescription: IPW Enrolled And Completed In New Term Error
labels.value: Student has completed the to-be-enrolled academic term.

labels.shortDescription: IPW not found in the selected term, the new term has an IPW, but not with #1
labels.value: Student not enrolled in the selected term, and the to-be-enrolled academic term is not scheduled in the upcoming Study Pathway Unit.

labels.shortDescription: No IPW found in the selected terms but one or more IPW is enrolled in other term
labels.value: Missing selected academic term and the to-be-enrolled academic term; student is already enrolled into another academic term.

labels.shortDescription: IPW found in the selected term but the next IPW is enrolled in a other term
labels.value: Student is already enrolled into another academic term.

labels.shortDescription: IPW not found in both term and the IPW with #1 is missing or already enrolled
labels.value: Selected and to-be-enrolled academic terms missing; student is missing #1 individual pathway or already enrolled in another academic term.

labels.shortDescription: IPWs found in both terms, but they are not subsequential
labels.value: Student is not scheduled to enroll in the to-be-enrolled academic term next semester.

labels.value: The number of {0} should be more than 0!

The record identifier {0} is invalid for {1}. If this problem persists, please contact a system administrator.

labels.shortDescription: Invalid object master-detail or lookup field for custom object.
labels.value: The object {0} does not have a master-detail or lookup field for {1}.

labels.shortDescription: Field type is not support. Supported type are Text, Lookup, Number or Checkbox.
labels.value: Field type of "{0}" is not supported. Supported types are Text, Lookup, Number or Checkbox.

labels.shortDescription: The operator "{0}" is invalid. Supported operators are {1}, {2}.
labels.value: The operator "{0}" is invalid. Supported operators are "{1}" or "{2}".

labels.shortDescription: The operator is invalid. Supported operators are =, != or isblank
labels.value: The operator "{0}" is invalid. Supported operators are "{1}", "{2}" or "{3}".

labels.shortDescription: Invalid value in {Field Matching Value}. Supported value are true or false.
labels.value: The value entered in "{0}" is invalid. Supported values are "true" or "false".

labels.shortDescription: The value entered in fieldname is invalid. Please provide a numeric value.
labels.value: The value entered in "{0}" is invalid. Please provide a numeric value.

labels.shortDescription: Invalid Study Session Enrollment Rules defined in Study Offering:
Invalid value defined in the field.

labels.value: Are you sure you want to cancel the selected job(s)?
The job(s) {0} cannot be cancelled as they are currently being processed, already completed, or have already been cancelled. Please remove them from the selection and try again.
The job(s) {0} cannot be retried as it is being processed or pending. Please remove them from selection and try again.
The job(s) {0} cannot be cancelled as they are already completed or cancelled. Please remove them from the selection and try again.
The job(s) were cancelled, but some executions may have already completed. Please check the logs for more details.

labels.value: Please select an '{0}' from the options below. Note that this action will {1} all program enrollments for the student for the selected academic term and may affect their ability to enroll or make changes.

Select an option to apply it to all students below.

labels.shortDescription: More than 1 field is found in the comment(s).
labels.value: More than one field mapping is found in the "{0}" formula field for translation. Ensure that only one field is specified in the comment(s), enclosed with '''{''' and ''}''. e.g., /*'{Name}'*/.

labels.value: Duplicate current academic term found in Individual Pathway.

labels.shortDescription: Invalid Format! Please define the sequence number by enclosing letters
labels.value: Invalid Format ({0})! Please define the sequence number by enclosing letters or numbers to repeat using '''{''' and '''}'''! For example: "STUD-'{0000}'."
System variable named "{0}" not found! Please create a new system variable and define its format.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release Campus Combobox Helptext
labels.value: This is required to filter the {0} table, displaying only the {0} related to the selected {1}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release Campus Combobox Label

labels.value: All {0} in the selected {1} for {2} will be updated with the Grade Release Status set to '{3}'. Please confirm that you want to proceed.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Grade Release Statuses Combobox Helptext
labels.value: The selected grade release status will be applied to the chosen batch of students.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Grade Release Statuses Combobox Label
Term Grade Release - Ien already release
labels.value: This Individual Enrollment is already in the selected release status "{0}".

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - IEN Originates From Credit Transfer Application
labels.value: This individual enrollment is being skipped for grade release because it originates from a {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Help Modal Overview
labels.value:
The Term Grade Release Wizard enables users to bulk release grades for a batch of students within a given term.

It consists of three screens, detailed below:

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release Selection Screen Description
labels.value: This will set the grade release status of the {0} in the selected {1} for {2} "{3}" to the specified status. Please select the following details to begin.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Selection Type Combobox Label
Term Grade Release - Selection Type Help Text
labels.value: You can select all {0} or manually select {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Help Modal Step 1
labels.value:
First Step:
Users must select the {0} for which they want to release grades. The study units table in this step includes all {0} that have {1} in the term using this wizard. By default, all {0} are selected. However, users can change the selection type to {2} to release grades only for specific {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Help Modal Step 2
labels.value:
Second Step:
Users must select the {0} for which they want to release grades. This table displays only the {0} related to the selected {1} within the term using this wizard. By default, all {0} are selected. Users can change the selection type to {2} to release grades only for specific {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release - Help Modal Step 3
labels.value:
Third Step:
Users will have to select the grade release status to apply to the chosen batch of student enrollments in this step.
Note: The wizard will skip records originating from {0} and records that have already been released with the selected grade release status.

After clicking the "Add Job" button in the third step, an Asynchronous Job Queue is created and placed in the queue. Users can navigate to the Job Queue records to check the status of the grade release process.

labels.shortDescription: Term Grade Release Modal Sub Title
Term Grade Release - Summary Record Count Details
labels.value: There are {0} {1} selected for grade release.
This wizard allows you to monitor the {0} status and start a new {0} job.
When a new job is added, it may take a few seconds before showing in the table below. You can click the Refresh button to refresh the table.
These are the {0} jobs in the queue:

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Campus Combobox Helptext
labels.value: This is required to filter the {0} table, displaying only the {0} related to the selected {1}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Campus Combobox Label
Term Offerings Cloning Clone Study Session Combobox Label
Term Offerings Cloning - Datatable Message For All Selection Type
labels.value:
You have selected the {0} selection type, which will include all available {1} records.
A total of {2} {1} records will be processed.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Datatable Message When Exceed Maximum Row Number
labels.value: The number of available {0} records has exceeded the maximum row limit of {1}. Please refine your filter to include all available {0} records in the table.
Please confirm that you want to proceed.
You have chosen to include {0}. Please note that the {1} will not be generated automatically. You will need to review the records and update the "{2}" option for each {3} to generate the events.
Depending on your selection, cloned {0} and {1} will either retain the original name or update the {2} name or alternate name (e.g., "{3}") with the target {2} (e.g., "{4}").
All {0} will be set to follow the start date ({1}) and end date ({2}) of {3} "{4}". You may update them after the records are cloned if needed.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Help Modal Overview
labels.value:
The {0} allows users to clone the {1} setup from one {2} to another.

It consists of three screens, detailed below:

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Retain Record Name Combobox Helptext
labels.value: If "{0}" is selected, the cloned {1}'s {2} name will be replaced with the target {1}'s {2} name. Otherwise, it keeps the original name. You can still edit it after cloning.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Retain Record Name Combobox Label
Term Offerings Cloning Retain Record Name Combobox Rename Option
Term Offerings Cloning Retain Record Name Combobox Retain Option
Term Offerings Cloning Selection Screen Description
labels.value: This will clone the {0} from the selected {1} to the current {1} "{2}". Please select the following details to begin.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Selection Type Combobox Label
Term Offerings Cloning - Selection Type Help Text
labels.value: You can select all {0} or manually select {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Help Modal Step 1
labels.value:
First Step:
Users must select the following details:
  • {0} to Clone From: Specifies the originating {0} which {1} will be cloned.
  • {2}: To define the {2} from which the {1} will be cloned.
  • {3}: Determines which {3} should be included in the cloning process. Users can choose between:
    • {4}: Includes all {3} that have {1} set up in the selected {0} and {2} (default selection).
    • {5}: Allows users to specify {3} for cloning.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Help Modal Step 2
labels.value:
Second Step:
Users must select the {0} they want to clone.
  • This step will only consider the {0} associated with the selected {1}, {2}, and {3} from Step 1.
  • By default, all {0} are selected. Users can switch to "{4}" to select specific {0}.

Note:
  • The {0} table is displayed only if "{4}" is chosen.
  • If the number of {0} exceeds the "Maximum Row Number for Custom Datatable" set in the system settings, some records may not appear. To avoid this, users may refine their selections in Step 1 (e.g., reducing the number of {1} to limit the number of {0} per batch).

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Help Modal Step 3
labels.value:
Third Step:
Users must select whether to clone all {0} of the selected {1}.
Please note that the {2} will not be generated automatically. Users will need to review the records and update the "{3}" option for each {4} to generate the events.

Users will need to choose whether to retain or rename the cloned record. Based on their selection, the cloned record will either keep the original name from which it was cloned, or replace the {5} name (or alternate name) with the target {5} name (or alternate name) for {0} and {1}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Help Modal Step 4
labels.value:
Fourth Step:
This screen provides a summary of the cloning details. Users should review the details before adding the job to the queue.

Important:
  • Date fields, including Start Date, End Date, Census Date, Pre-enrollment Open Date, Enrollment Open Date, and Enrollment Close Date, will be left empty in the cloned records. The calculated dates will align with the setup of the target term, and users can update these fields after cloning if needed.
After clicking the "Add Job" button in the third step, an Asynchronous Job Queue is created and placed in the queue. Users can navigate to the Job Queue records to check the status of the term offerings cloning process.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Modal Sub Title

labels.value:
{0} to Be Cloned From: {2}
Current {0}: {3}
{1}: {4}
{5}: {6}
Record Count: {7}
Clone {8}: {9}
Retain {10} Name: {11}
Retain {8} Name: {12}
You have chosen not to clone the {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning - Summary Record Count Details
Term Offerings Cloning Term Combobox Helptext
labels.value: This determines which {0} the {1} should be cloned from.

labels.shortDescription: Term Offerings Cloning Term Combobox Label

labels.value: This wizard allows you to monitor the {0} status and start a new {0} job.
When a new job is added, it may take a few seconds before showing in the table below. You can click the Refresh button to refresh the table.
These are the {0} jobs in the queue:

labels.shortDescription: Warning message when contact already enrolled into the study unit.
labels.value: Auto Unit Enrollment for {0} {1} for {2} {3} was skipped because an {4} in the same {0} was found for this student {5}.

labels.shortDescription: Error message when no applicable study pathway term found.
labels.value: No applicable {0} found for {1}. This might be because the {2}'s {3} does not have a {0} with term number {4}.

labels.shortDescription: Error Msg for IPE {IPE Name} due to no available places remaining in {SOF Name}
labels.value: Auto Unit Enrollment failed for {0} {1} due to no available {2} or the {2} not having available places remaining for {3} {4}.

labels.shortDescription: Error when the IPW is linked to an IPE that does not have the IPF populated.
labels.value: Auto Unit Enrollment failed for {0} {1} because {2} was not found.

labels.shortDescription: Term Student Enrollment - No job selected

labels.value: There are no Individual Program Enrollments available for processing in both term enrollment and unit enrollment. Please return to the previous screen to select another available batch of students. Alternatively, you may fix the data if the Number of Students Excluded is greater than 0.

The automatic enrollment wizard enables enrollment for both terms and recommended study units, with configurable options that allow users to customize the scope of enrollments based on specific needs.
This wizard will be placed on the study term record page or will function as an action button on the study record page. Users should initiate this wizard from the term they wish to enroll the student into.

It consists of four screens, which are detailed below:

This step is used to determine the batch of students to be processed for automatic enrollment:
  • Past Study Term: A picklist displaying a list of study terms. Users must select the term in which the students are currently enrolled and should undergo automatic enrollment.
  • Target Campus: Specifies the campus for the batch of students to be enrolled automatically.
  • Target Study Program: Determines the available options in the 'Target Study Intakes' list.
  • Target Study Intakes: Displays the study intakes for the batch of students. This list will show study intakes that match the selected campus and study program.
Based on these selections, the wizard will determine which batch of students will proceed with automatic enrollment into the new study term.

First Step:
Users will configure four options on this step:
  • Past Study Term
  • Target Campus
  • Target Study Program
  • Target Study Intakes
Behind the scenes, these selected values will determine the Individual Program Enrollment records considered in the automatic enrollment process.
The Individual Program Enrollments that match the selected target campus and target study intakes will go through an eligibility check to determine whether they can be enrolled in the new term.

The results of this check will be displayed on the second screen.

This step provides an overview of the number of eligible and excluded student records for each selected study intake:

Eligible Student Records: This column displays the student record count, including the following scenarios:
  • Students who have not yet enrolled in the new term and are eligible for automatic enrollment into both the term and recommended study units (if applicable).
  • Students who have already enrolled in the new term but are only eligible for recommended study unit enrollment (if applicable).
Excluded Student Records: This column displays the number of student records that will be excluded from the automatic enrollment process, along with the reason for exclusion.

If you would like to include the excluded student records in the automatic enrollment process, please review and correct the data before rerunning the wizard.

Second Step:
This step provides an overview of:
  • The number of students eligible for automatic enrollment
  • The students excluded from the process, along with reasons for exclusion and sample records
Users may resolve issues with excluded records before proceeding. Only eligible student records will be considered for both term enrollment and recommended study unit enrollment.

This step is used to confirm whether the selected students will be enrolled in the recommended study units defined in their study pathway.
Only the study pathway units that directly specify a study unit will be considered for enrollment, and the study pathway units that point to a group will not be included.

Select 'Yes': A checkbox will appear, allowing the user to choose whether to bypass the requirement rules.
  • If bypassed, all students will be eligible to enroll in the recommended study units regardless of whether the requirements have been met.
  • If not bypassed, recommended study units enrollment will adhere to the rules. Students who do not meet the requirements will not be enrolled in the course, and an error will be logged.
Select 'No': The wizard will enroll eligible students in the new term without enrolling them in recommended study units.

Third Step:
This step allows users to confirm whether the selected students will be enrolled in the recommended study units defined in their study pathway.
Only the study pathway units that directly specify a study unit will be considered for enrollment, and the study pathway units that point to a group will not be included.

If the user selects 'No', the wizard will only enroll eligible students in the new term, without enrolling them in recommended study units.

This step displays the final count of eligible student records:
  • Eligible for New Term Enrollment: These students will be enrolled in the new term, including unit enrollment if applicable.
  • Eligible for Unit Enrollment: This represents the total number of students eligible for recommended study unit enrollment. It includes both students enrolling in a new term and those already enrolled in the term before this process was run.
After submitting the final decision by clicking the Start button, an Asynchronous Job Queue will be created and placed in the queue. Users can navigate to the Job Queue records to check the status of the auto-enrollment process. Any errors or warnings encountered during the process will be logged in the Job Queue records.

Final Step:
This step displays the final count of students who will undergo the automatic enrollment process.

After confirming the final count and initiating the process, an Asynchronous Job Queue will be created and placed in the queue. Users can navigate to the Job Queue records to check the status of the auto-enrollment process.

For more information, please refer to https://help.rioeducation.com/rio-education-v4-lightning-web-components/v4-term-student-enrollment.

labels.shortDescription: Term Student Enrollment Modal Sub Title

labels.value: This wizard allows you to monitor the {0} status and start a new {0} job.
When a new job is added, it may take a few seconds before showing in the table below. You can click the Refresh button to refresh the table.
These are the {0} jobs in the queue:

labels.shortDescription: The Transaction Payment record Id is required
labels.value: The {0} record Id is required in the transactionPayment for update.

labels.shortDescription: Translation Map not found in object!
Please confirm that you would like to unenroll.
labels.value: Please confirm that you would like to unenroll from {0}.
Unhandled scenario. Please check the knowledge base to identify the data issue.

labels.shortDescription: The unit enrollment has exceeded the maximum number of attempts allowed.
labels.value: The unit enrollment ({0}) has exceeded the maximum number of attempts allowed ({1}).
Unknown error. Please try to refresh the page again. If the problem still persists, please contact your system administrator.
The selected {0} is not qualified for the {1}.
You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave this page? Unsaved changes will be lost.

labels.shortDescription: You are not allowed to update record.
labels.value: You are not allowed to update the {0} ({1}).

labels.shortDescription: Click on Confirm to view the Pathway Visualizer
labels.value: Click on Confirm to view the Pathway Visualizer

labels.shortDescription: Are you sure to withdraw from all the following unit enrollments?
labels.value:
Are you sure to withdraw from all the following unit enrollments?
Please note that some unit enrollments might be referenced or shared if the student has multiple program enrollments.

labels.shortDescription: Please confirm that you would like to withdraw from the pre-enrollment.
labels.value: Please confirm that you would like to withdraw from the pre-enrollment for {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Withdrawn from the pre-enrollment for the study offering name.
Please confirm that you would like to withdraw.
labels.value: Please confirm that you would like to withdraw from the waiting list for {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Withdrawn from the waiting list for study offering name.
labels.value: Withdrawn from the waiting list for {0}.
Multiple matching {0} found. Please update the {0} manually.
No matching {0} found. Please update the {0} manually.
No active {0} found. Please update the {1} manually.
The field "{0}" in object "{1}" is not of boolean type.

labels.shortDescription: Too many parent objects in transaction
labels.value: You cannot process more than five different parent objects in a single transaction. Please reduce the number of parent object types that need to be updated. Related {0} objects in this transaction: {1}
There is a mixture of {0}’ {1} in an {2}.
An error occurred while setting field "{0}": {1}

labels.shortDescription: The object name (record name) is locked
Are you sure you want to submit for verification?
labels.value: Are you sure you want to submit for verification?

labels.shortDescription: The Individual Achievement (IAC name) is submitted for verification.
The Individual Achievement (IAC name) is already submitted for verification.
labels.value: The {0} ({1}) is already submitted for verification.

labels.shortDescription: Complete all the requirements before submit for verification!
labels.value: Complete all the requirements before submit for verification!

labels.shortDescription: Warning to indicates that it will be updated to not verified and resubmit (iac)
labels.value:
After updating the verified {0} ({1}), the {0} will be reverted to Not Verified and is required to resubmit for verification.
Are you sure you want to update the {0}?

labels.shortDescription: Warning to indicates that it will be updated to not verified and resubmit (iau)
labels.value:
After creating the {0}, the {0} will be reverted to Not Verified and is required to resubmit for verification.
Are you sure you want to update the {0}?

labels.shortDescription: The record is saved. Please review and provide the required supporting documents
labels.value: The record is saved. Please review and provide the required supporting documents.

labels.shortDescription: Label of Agreement Recognition Unit field in Individual Achievement Unit
Message for error when no matched or default wizard config found
labels.value: No matched or default wizard config found. If the problem still persists, please contact your system administrator.

labels.shortDescription: Message for error when no matched wizard config
labels.value: No matched wizard config found. If the problem still persists, please contact your system administrator.
